name: Velvet_Void
psychological_stake:
- The most humiliating thing that happened to me this week was when I tripped over my own boots and spilled coffee on a stranger at Equinox on 14th.
- The thing about my body I'm convinced everyone notices but actually no one does is my uneven eyelids that start every conversation.
- My last sexual accident was when I accidentally texted 'nice moves' to my therapist instead of my date.
- The kink I've never said out loud to anyone is being serenaded by an off-key saxophonist in a dimly lit subway station.
- The substance I leaned on harder than I should have last month was expired cough syrup just to get through an art critique I wasn't prepared for.
- The most embarrassing impulse purchase on my last bank statement is a sequined cape I thought would make me look mysterious but just makes me itchy.
- If you opened my browser history at 3am last Tuesday you'd find 'how to become a full-time hermit' and a twenty-minute video on potato peeling techniques.
- The last lie I told on a dating profile was saying I 'enjoy rock climbing' when I've only climbed onto my couch with difficulty.
- The most undignified thing my body did in public recently was hiccuping so loudly during a quiet moment at Communion in Oakland that the band stopped playing.
- The thing I do alone in my apartment that I'd be humiliated to be filmed doing is reciting lines from 90s sitcoms while trying to balance a book on my head.
- The single object in my bedroom I could not explain to a stranger is a mannequin torso draped in fairy lights that I talk to when drunk.
- The last time I cried was in the canned goods aisle of Trader Joe's after I dropped a jar of pickles on my foot.
- My last named ex was Kyle the gentle barista and it ended because he said I was 'too intense' after I started planning our imaginary wedding.
- The lowest professional moment of the last year was when my phone autocorrected 'artistic genius' to 'artistic anus' in an email to a gallery owner.
- The thing I genuinely believe will happen to me in the next two years that everyone else would call delusional is becoming the muse for a major art movement named after me.

age_and_demographics.bio_lie: knows exactly what she's doing. has reviewed her own patterns extensively, cited them in therapy, and is doing them again. eyes open. fully consenting. send help anyway.
age_and_demographics.tragic_reality: She dropped out of art school in her second year when the scholarship ran out, withdrawing on the same afternoon she got her first piercing; the only decision of that day she was fully confident in, and still is; then worked three jobs simultaneously for eight months on four hours of sleep, eating takeout standing up between shifts. She built systems to manage the chaos when her job, relationship, and living situation all went sideways within the same four months at twenty-two, and she kept those systems after the situations resolved; that same year her mother died, and the first of her tattoos was for her, the second for someone she wanted to remember, until by the fifth she stopped requiring a reason, each one tied to a year she still thinks about. Her texture accumulated over time as a series of datable events; a bad burn at seventeen that healed wrong, stretch marks at twenty, the accident at twenty-four; and she doesn't sand any of it down for anyone's comfort. She saved for four months working at a coffee shop and bought her boots the day she received her first tattoo commission that paid what the work was worth, has had them resoled once, and won't replace them.

birthplace_and_origin.tragic_reality: Born in a small town in Iowa, the only thing going for it was a 24-hour diner she frequented alone.

childhood_milieu.bio_lie: Grew up in an enchanting, art-filled home with a fridge full of creativity.
childhood_milieu.tragic_reality: Spent afternoons drawing unicorns on the back of supermarket receipts while her parents argued in the other room.

parental_dynamics.bio_lie: My parents? Free spirits in love and light.
parental_dynamics.tragic_reality: Father left when she was six; mother turned the living room into a yoga studio and forgot Velvet’s eighth birthday.

early_education_scars.bio_lie: Excelled in every subject, especially recess.
early_education_scars.tragic_reality: Bullied in middle school for her mismatched socks and lack of lunch money. Once got locked in a supply closet for an entire afternoon.

higher_education.bio_lie: Art school alum, '03. Magna cum laude. Color theory master.
higher_education.tragic_reality: Dropped out of art school after losing her scholarship, found solace in body modifications and freelance gigs.

formative_intimacies.bio_lie: Heartbreaker. Only serious relationships with fictional characters.
formative_intimacies.tragic_reality: First love was a pen pal she never met; he ghosted her after eight months of intense letter exchanges.

career_debut.bio_lie: Launched an avant-garde gallery in a Soho loft with champagne fountains.
career_debut.tragic_reality: Worked as a barista at a sketchy coffee shop while selling her doodles on Etsy.

current_profession.bio_lie: Influencer. Curates vibes for a living.
current_profession.tragic_reality: Freelance graphic designer, often unpaid, always underappreciated. Moonlights doing data entry to make rent.

financial_hygiene.bio_lie: Invested in cryptocurrency before it was cool.
financial_hygiene.tragic_reality: Overdrafted three times last month; treats payday like a religious holiday.

domestic_milieu.bio_lie: Minimalist loft with a view of the universe.
domestic_milieu.tragic_reality: Lives in a cluttered studio apartment with a leaky faucet and mismatched furniture from curbsides.

social_circle.bio_lie: Breakfast club with Andy Warhol’s ghost and Banksy.
social_circle.tragic_reality: Mostly acquaintances; her closest friend is her overly affectionate, elderly landlady, Mrs. Fischer.

recent_ex.bio_lie: Dated a rising tech mogul, broke it off for artistic differences.
recent_ex.tragic_reality: Broke up with an Uber driver after realizing she was just a free ride to art shows.

career_low.bio_lie: Criticized for being too ahead of her time.
career_low.tragic_reality: Accidentally submitted a blank canvas to an art competition and didn't notice until the awards night.

delusional_plan.bio_lie: Planning a year-long retreat to commune with muses in the Himalayas.
delusional_plan.tragic_reality: Thinks she can host a multi-day festival in her apartment celebrating 'Chiaroscuro' while charging admission.

hyperfixations.tragic_reality: Collects broken mirrors from thrift stores, convinced they hold keys to alternate realities.

ideological_posture.bio_lie: Post-modernist anarchist with a hint of zen nihilism.
ideological_posture.tragic_reality: Has read only the summaries of political manifestos; forms opinions based on horoscope columns.

digital_footprint.tragic_reality: Her Twitter is a chaotic mess of half-baked thoughts and deleted posts about conspiracy theories.

physical_dysmorphia.tragic_reality: Convinced her left elbow looks like an angry potato and covers it with band-aids, even in summer.

dependencies.bio_lie: Just a touch of caffeine and daily affirmations.
dependencies.tragic_reality: Relies on instant noodles and energy drinks to maintain her nocturnal art binges.
